Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
Laboratory results are largely within normal limits. Mildly decreased total CO₂ may suggest a tendency toward mild metabolic acidosis, but clinical correlation is needed. Cholesterol and LDL are slightly elevated, indicating increased cardiovascular risk; lipid management may be considered. Renal function, glucose control, and urine microalbumin/creatinine ratio are normal. No acute laboratory concerns identified.
